candidates.notes: All setup_env pass criteria met. conda env sibyl_CRA created with Python 3.11. PyTorch 2.5.1+cu121 with CUDA on 4x RTX 4090.

K-FAC control completed on Pythia-70M (v2: corrected K-FAC factors, added Raw Dot IF, damping sweep). CRITICAL FINDING: On counterfact (genuine attribution), RepSim outperforms K-FAC IF by 17.4pp -- H6 strongly confirmed. K-FAC improves +12.0pp over Diagonal IF, showing Hessian quality helps but cannot close the representation gap. On toxicity, ALL parameter-space IF methods (including Raw Dot with NO Hessian at all: AUPRC=0.94) achieve near-perfect AUPRC, while RepSim gets 0.74. This is a gradient norm artifact: unsafe samples have systematically higher loss gradients (Cohen's d=2.66 for raw dot). K-FAC damping sweep (1e-2 to 1e-4) shows AUPRC is insensitive to damping on toxicity (all 0.9915). REVISED INTERPRETATION: Toxicity AUPRC tests gradient magnitude detection, not attribution quality. H6 confirmed on genuine attribution (counterfact). CRA thesis holds with task-dependent nuance.

h6_decision_gate.revision_reason: Toxicity AUPRC is not a valid H6 test because even Diagonal IF (no Hessian correction) achieves 0.9845, showing the task tests gradient norm magnitude not attribution quality. K-FAC adds only 0.7pp over Diagonal on toxicity. On counterfact (genuine factual attribution), H6 passes strongly: RepSim > K-FAC IF by 16.7pp.
evidence: Raw Dot IF AUPRC = 0.9401 (near-perfect with NO Hessian at all, Cohen's d=2.66)
Diagonal IF AUPRC = 0.9768 (near-perfect with minimal curvature correction)
K-FAC IF AUPRC = 0.9915 (only +1.5pp over Diagonal -- Hessian quality barely matters)
K-FAC damping insensitive: 1e-2, 1e-3, 1e-4 all give AUPRC=0.9915 on toxicity
TRAK AUPRC = 0.7776 (lower because CountSketch projection loses gradient norm info)
On counterfact: RepSim (1.438) > K-FAC IF (1.264) > Raw Dot (1.200) > TRAK (1.206) > Diagonal IF (1.144)

key_findings.H4_status: DIRECTIONALLY_SUPPORTED (gradient spectrum decays much faster than representation spectrum; pilot N=100 prevents testing [256, 1024] range directly)
key_findings.H9_status: INCONCLUSIVE (condition numbers unreliable at N=100 < d=512; rep covariance is rank-deficient so condition number is meaningless; directional finding holds: gradient is more concentrated)
key_findings.core_finding: Full-model gradients are EXTREMELY concentrated (top-5 captures 85.6% of variance vs 34.9% for representations). This is the core FM1 evidence: gradient signal lives in a very low-rank subspace.
key_findings.condition_number_correction: Original rep condition (3e10) included near-zero eigenvalues from rank deficiency (N=100 < d=512). Non-zero-only condition (4.5e7) is still unreliable because smallest non-zero eigenvalue at rank boundary is noise-dominated.

risks_identified.risk: Toxicity AUPRC tests gradient norm magnitude, not attribution quality
risks_identified.impact: CRA claims about FM1 may not apply to binary detection tasks where gradient norms are informative
risks_identified.mitigation: Frame toxicity as a separate phenomenon; focus H6 evidence on counterfact; full-scale N=10K may reveal different pattern

risks_identified.risk: K-FAC IF improves 6.9pp over Diagonal on counterfact
risks_identified.impact: Hessian quality does help somewhat -- CRA should acknowledge this partial FM1 fix
risks_identified.mitigation: Position K-FAC as partial FM1 fix that still cannot close the 16.7pp gap with representation space

risks_identified.risk: Small pilot N=100 may distort metrics
risks_identified.mitigation: Full experiment uses complete training pool (5K-10K samples)

risks_identified.risk: H9 INCONCLUSIVE: Condition numbers unreliable at pilot scale (N=100 < d=512)
risks_identified.impact: Cannot confirm or deny H9 specific thresholds (rep_cond < 100, grad_cond > 10^4) at pilot scale. Rep covariance is rank-deficient (rank 99 in d=512), making condition number meaningless.
risks_identified.mitigation: Need N >= 2048 for proper test. The directional finding (gradient more concentrated than representation) is robust and supports FM1. Use Ledoit-Wolf shrinkage for more robust condition estimation at full scale. Consider reframing H9 in terms of spectral concentration rather than raw condition numbers.

risks_identified.risk: Full-model gradient r_eff=10 suggests signal is in ~10 dimensions, not ~d=512
risks_identified.impact: H4 predicted r_eff in [256, 1024]. If confirmed at full scale, the 'r_eff ~ O(d)' claim needs revision to 'r_eff << d'.
risks_identified.mitigation: This actually STRENGTHENS the FM1 argument (signal dilution is even worse). Reframe H4: the point is that gradient signal is low-rank relative to parameter dimension B, not that it equals d.

kfac_spectral_summary.note: Very high condition numbers in activation covariance confirm rank deficiency in gradient space (FM1 evidence)
